entry.title: Re: Moving SAP S/4HANA Change Management to the 21st Century using DevOps Methodologies
entry.content: 97% AI generated, not bad.

entry.title: No Authorization to post inventory differences
entry.content:
I receive this error, when I try to post Inventory differences.

"You cannot post the inventory difference for this item.
Terminate this processing mode and choose Inventory Count -> Enter to enter the count results without difference posting.
To post the difference for this item, contact a person who is authorized to post higher inventory differences.
Message no. MM_IMRAP_PI053"

 



I am Authorized to all, as far as I can see.

What could be the reason?

Best Regards
Manuel

entry.title: How to identify the usage of SAP Open Hub license?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I need to understand the usage of SAP Open Hub license? and Is there any way to identify the integration trigging Open Hub usage?

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/remote-services#csrf-token-handling

entry.title: Re: How to determine the RFC type
entry.content: thanks for the information

entry.title: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP AIF you can define functions which trigger a specific transaction or report. From what I can see one can only use the 4 variables as input values for the function. In my case I have a sales order number in a specific Idoc filed, but not available as one of the variables in the error message.

Is it possible to use the value of the Idoc field as input for the function. In my case I want to start VA02/VA03 with the sales order number in one of the Idoc fields.

Thanks in advance,

Pascal.

entry.title: Binary Attachment file through Form-Data not working in sap cpi
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on a scenario where ServiceNow is sending attachment(any format img/pdf/ppt/.xls etc) in Base64 format to SAP CPI. SAP CPI decoder used and converted to binary format which needs to be sent to JIRA Api which accepts form data. 

While triggering the same binary file(taken from Trace) from POSTMAN it is posting successfully and the attachment is displayed in expected format. But the same thing is giving 500 Error from SAP CPI.

 

 

 

 

 

The flow is mentioned below where I am using the decoder to convert to binary. Setting multi-part header through Content modifier but it is not working.

I tried using the groovy scripts from the below two blogs to solve my issue but no luck.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-cpi-forwarding-raw-image-data-through-integration-flow/qaq-p/11936736

Error Details 

Please guide me how can this be resolved.

Thanks.

Best Regards,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: Retrieve metadata failed because the OData server returned HTTP code, 401
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Could you help me?
When accessing the cockpit of the Mobile Service and navigating to "Mobile Connectivity," it is returning:

"An error occurred while communicating with the OData server to retrieve the result of request 'https:/xxx/DEST_SAM2205_ONLINE_PPROP/$metadata'.
Error caused by: [-100010] Retrieve metadata failed because the OData server returned HTTP code, 401, with message: Anmeldung fehlgeschlagen."

However, when performing the connection tests with SAP Gateway/OData via the SAP Gateway Client (/N/IWFND/GW_CLIENT) and using the GW user provided by the client, it returns a 200 connection status, as shown in the image.

When using the same GW user in the Mobile Service for the connection, it fails to authenticate, returning a 401 error: "Authorization Denied."

The GET request to the backend http://xxx/sap/opu/odata/MERP/SAP_ONLINE_LOOKUP_EXT_2205 was completed in 158 minutes. The backend's HTTP status is 401.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: How to show a custom message in story while the table values load from live connection?
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created a story where I'm pulling the data from SAP source. There are multiple widgets in the story and few tables and charts are using the SAP source to pull the data into the story. My requirement is to show a custom message to the users until all the data is refreshed in the story. 

Anyone who has faced similar issue or knows the workaround in this case. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Enhance your SAP Datasphere Experience with API Access
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
hi @thorebedey 

I'm a little curious now:
The post was created in April 2024. Is there perhaps already a WebAPI that is as powerful as CLI?

Maybe I was looking in the wrong places because I couldn't find any information about a WebAPI.

Thank you in advance,

Marco

entry.title: Re: MIRO ERROR
entry.content: No I put it but still the same error

entry.title: Re: Sharepoint CSV-File integration in Datasphere's Data...
entry.content:
Hi @Michael_BE!

Thanks for the info, but we were able to reproduce exactly this incorrect behavior.
After an initial response from SAP, it seems that there are challenges with special characters etc.

Unfortunately, we cannot assume that our customer also has an English version of Sharepoint.

But nevertheless, were you able to use files from sub-sites?

Thanks in advance,

Marco

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
Hi,

There are a lot of limitations that we have observed while testing in our development enviourmnet, while some of them will be resolved in the coming update of 1H 2025 for the other limitations there is no update on when they will have a similar feature in the New Learning Homepage. The following are the limitations we found that should have something similar in New learning Homepage :

  • The "Launch Proxy" action (Learning Administration > People > Users > Actions) will display the legacy Learning home page, even with the "New Learning Experience" toggle on for the user being proxied.
  • Blank searching or browsing in Enhanced Search is not available. Minimum 2 characters are needed for a search.
  • New Items, curricula and programs and changes to existing items, curricula and programs will take up to 24 hours to be available in the production system and in the stage environments, items added to a library may take up to a week to be visible to users. 
  • Calendar View is not available in the Find Learning page.
  • All sections such as Required Learning, Important Learning, etc. will only show up to 8 cards. The View All option must be used to see more.
  • Optional Surveys and Follow-Up surveys won’t be available. (Surveys which are required for item completion will show on the new home page. Only Course Feedback surveys impact item completion.)
  • The item relaunch warning that appears when the item was completed in the past is currently not supported.
  • In the bookmarks, sections will reappear even after dismissed when the page is refreshed/ users navigate away and back to homepage/ New Learning Experience toggle is turned off and on again.
  • Optional items that are part of curriculum won't be displayed on the new learning page.
  • Highlight as "Featured", "New" and "Revised" items won’t be displayed in the items as there's a flag filter in Enhanced Search page to filter by the same.
  • Setting Priority is not supported.
  • When email notifications are disabled and the "Send Reminder" button available in the Team View area is clicked, emails are generated and queued. If the queued emails aren't purged and the emails are enabled, they're sent.
  • Thumbnail images not appearing for Required Learning and Important Learning section

Could anybody let me know if there is any timeline for migrating these features from Legacy to NLH?

entry.title: GitHub announcement invalid links to release notes
entry.content:
Whenever a new release is published it contains links to:

---

Documentation

In the OpenUI5 SDK, you can find a summary of what’s new in this release:
https://sdk.openui5.org/1.132.0/#/topic/99ac68a5b1c3416ab5c84c99fefa250d


Change log

https://sdk.openui5.org/1.132.0/#releasenotes.html

---

These link never work for me and it seems they never work for anyone. Can something be done to fix those to point to working release notes and summary instead.

entry.title: Explore The Industry of Custom Packaging Boxes
entry.content: Go here to explore The benefits of The Custom Boxes

entry.title: Re: Can SAP BW 7.0 source data from SAP S/4HANA using SAPI?
entry.content:
In S/4HANA many extractors have been replaced with CDS Views, which can be extracted via ODP. You should check for the specific extractors, if they are still available in the corresponding S/4HANA version.

Also check:

2481315 Operational Data Provisioning (ODP): Extracting from SAP Systems to SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA – Availability and Limitations

entry.title: Re: Configuring the proxy CO_CRM_ISX_CC_CATALOG_SERVICES
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please have a look also on the following SAP note :

Title: 2970772 - Wrong selection of Port Definition for CRM CC web services
Link: https://me.sap.com/notes/2970772/E

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: Unlock Your Future: Prove Your ABAP Cloud Readiness Today!
entry.content:
In the fast-evolving tech landscape, staying ahead means embracing the future. And in the world of ABAP development, that future is ABAP Cloud.

What is ABAP Cloud?

ABAP Cloud is the cutting-edge evolution in ABAP development. It's your gateway to creating cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions that stand the test of time. Imagine harnessing the power of Core Data Services (CDS) for seamless data modelling, the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) for streamlined service development, and a cloud-optimized ABAP language that makes your business logic soar.

This all-encompassing programming model caters to both the SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, working harmoniously across public cloud, private cloud, and on-premise environments. It's not just the next step—it's the future.

Get Certified and Stand Out

Ready to showcase your ABAP Cloud expertise? The SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud certification is your ticket to proving your prowess. But how do you prepare for this transformative exam?

Explore Your Training Options

Whether you're a fan of interactive classroom experiences or prefer the flexibility of e-learning, SAP has you covered.

If you appreciate the benefits of classroom training, you can prepare for the certification with the classroom training courses S4D400 (Basic ABAP Programming), S4D401 (Intermediate ABAP Programming) and S4D430 (Data Modelling in ABAP Dictionary and ABAP Core Data Services) - as shown in the curriculum map below. Building on S4D430, the free e-learning Practicing Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud covers the remaining content required for the exam.

 

 


 

In-Depth Knowledge: Even though the 5-day classroom training course S4D437 (Building Transactional Apps with the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) - also shown in the curriculum map - isn't included in the exam, it's a goldmine for those serious about mastering the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. We highly recommend it!

E-Learning Flexibility: For those who prefer learning at their own pace, there are excellent alternatives. Instead of attending the classroom training courses S4D400, S4D401 and S4D430, you can alternatively work through the free e-learning Acquiring Core ABAP Skills. It covers the entirety of the three classroom training courses, allowing you to study whenever and wherever you want.

Mix and Match Your Learning Path

Flexibility is key. Combine classroom and e-learning approaches to fit your schedule and learning style. For example, attend S4D400 in person, then switch to the e-learning Acquiring Core ABAP Skills to cover the content of S4D401 and S4D430 — it's entirely your call. The e-learning content is carefully designed to mirror classroom training, so you won't miss a beat.

Your Path, Your Future

Chart your own course to ABAP Cloud certification. Take control of your learning journey, prove your skills, and unlock unparalleled opportunities in the tech world.

Ready to embark on this exciting journey? Your future in ABAP Cloud awaits!

This article has been created by Christoph Knecht

entry.title: Re: How to Import /BIC/* tables"s data between P and...
entry.content:
Or if needed, one can load data from one BW system to another:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/ca7284cfe8074eeabd09011d432f2070.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Anyone who have encountered the configuration of CRT which is different from the fiscal period?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Anyone who have configured the Annual Cumulation in CRT for certain wage types which is different from the fiscal period? I have already maintained V_T54C1,V_T54C2,V_T54C3,V_T56C3,V_T54C3,V_T54C1. But the cumulation is not refreshed after March 31. The cumulation period is from April to March.

Your input is very much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Total Deduction in the HRFOMS Payslip not correct
entry.content:

2 - As implied by XxEMIxX, double-check the configuration of your payslip

entry.title: Re: Validation Policy in Ariba Guided Buying
entry.content:
Hi Ariba experts!
I have a question regarding policies in Ariba Guided Buying. Can answers to policies be used in Ariba reporting?

E.g: I have a justification policy where users must write down their cellphone number. I wan to create a report in Ariba, were I can see the orders created and the different mobile numbers of the users who have purchased.

Is this possible?

Thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Project Change Request Feature/APP/Functionality (Portfolio & Project Management)
entry.content:
Dear All,

I was wondering if there are any standard functionalities/fiori apps that can support on the Project Change Request Task.
The question here revolves around having a tool that can handle change requests on a project (PPM Item and/or Initiative) in regards to /re-planning/new estimations on the approved scope and inherent budget (financial cost and hours), timeline (milestones & decision points). - This would imply the creation of the request, approval flow (authorizations), data update, (documentation storage?), but also some reporting layers e,g. pending changes, approved changes, rejected changes, number of changes per item, etc. 

I think I've seen some posts at some point about a similar topic - but in SAP Commercial PM. Hence I was wondering if this functionality is readily/standard available for the Portfolio & Project Management module, or if there is a possibility for it to be created.

Thank you in advance! 
Best regards

Bogdan

entry.content:
Hi gurus,

When i try to post a GI by RF transaction for EWM advance production i get a bug but also i receive in the inbox the next message error:

RF consumption

Error in inbox

Any ideas what could be the cause of this error?

Thanks,

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Understanding the rollup hierarchy method of planning
entry.content:
Hi Xavier,

Thank you for all your efforts and support!

The issue turned out to be that the "Active Users Only" box was checked when I viewed the Employee History. It’s indeed a simple detail, but now I’ve learned from it. Once again, thank you!

 

BR, Erika

entry.title: Re: How to identify the usage of SAP Open Hub license?
entry.content:
It's going to depend on what you are doing, and if you are doing anything that falls in line with this use explanation then you would likely be triggering it - 

Use

The open hub destination allows you to distribute data from a SAP BW∕4HANA system to non-SAP data marts, analytical applications, and other applications. It allows you to ensure controlled distribution across several systems.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Managing Dynamic Location Assignments in SAP IBP (same part different locations)
entry.content:
Hi,
Before I formulate any thought, can I ask if you have a sales and operations planning license and if you're using a production source header?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: sharing just the bookmark and restrict accessing the original Story.
entry.content: Hi Bouirik, I had the same idea to restrict access in a live data connection story (using and sharing specific bookmarks set as default to diferents users), but faced the same problem of yours, original story is still accesible and can't be hidden. Did you find any solution within SAC or did yo manage Data access from roles in SAP?

Danilo

entry.title: What Are the Biggest Challenges in the Packaging Boxes Industry, and How Can They Be Addressed?
entry.content:
The packaging industry plays a vital role in protecting products, enhancing branding, and ensuring sustainability. However, it faces significant challenges such as:

  1. Sustainability: With growing concerns about environmental impact, how can packaging companies balance cost-effectiveness with eco-friendly solutions?
  2. Customization: As consumer demands for personalized Packaging grow, what tools or technologies can businesses adopt to meet these requirements efficiently?
  3. Durability vs. Cost: Ensuring the durability of packaging while keeping production costs low is a persistent issue. What materials or techniques are best for achieving this balance?
  4. Design and Innovation: How can brands create innovative designs that stand out while maintaining functionality?
  5. Supply Chain Disruptions: The industry has faced material shortages and logistical challenges. What strategies can companies adopt to minimize delays and maintain product quality?

If you’re looking for solutions, companies like The Custom Boxes offer innovative and cost-effective packaging solutions. They specialize in custom boxes that cater to branding, durability, and eco-friendly demands.

Let’s discuss strategies, tools, and real-world solutions to tackle these challenges and ensure growth in the packaging industry. What has been your experience?

entry.title: cancel production receipt

hello good morning, i have one question :

thank

entry.title: Re: DMEE xml output BKREF missing
entry.content:
Solved out.

The problem was in the condition with the superordinate node - there was a dependancy on the value of a Reference ID.

entry.title: Business Network for Logistics - Testing BN4L APIs with SAP Business Accelerator Hub
entry.content:
Shippers, Carriers and other data contributors often spend a lot of time trying to understand and use the various BN4L APIs.   SAP Business Accelerator Hub has all the answers!   In this blog I will show some hints and tips about how to work with SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

There are many BN4L APIs. In this example I will focus on the API for Freight Order Confirmations.

Open SAP Business Accelerator Hub and search for the API at  https://api.sap.com/
For example I search for:

Voila!

Now that I have found the API I will spend some time reading all the documentation  (or skip the documentation and just dive straight into the testing, but Reading the documentation will help!!).

I've read the documentation (honest) and I'm now ready to test the API.  First I need to enter some information about my BN4L test environment (i.e. my BN4L test carrier tenant).

Before creating the environment, go to your BN4L test tenant and get the Service Credentials for Client ID and Client Secret.  At the time of writing they can be found in the System Connections App:

Copy/Paste to the Hub and Save: 

Enter the required Parameters. You will probably already know the LBN IDs, freight Order number and S/4 system. I got these from a previous message that sent a freight order request to a carrier but you can also get them from elsewhere.

 

The Body will be be prefilled with all possible parameters.  In my example I simply add the carrier LBN# and 'CN 'for Confirmation.   Now click Run:

Hopefully you now see a 200 Success message and a Confirmation appears in your systems.
Alternatively, you might see an error message with clear instructions about the problem 😎

 

 

Conclusion and Next Steps

I hope this blog helps you on your BN4L journey! 
Test the other BN4L APIs.
Use the example Code Snippets in your own systems.

entry.title: #10 - Review Cut-Over Plan - And The Importance for a Successful Go Live
entry.content:
A successful project Go Live is a significant milestone for any organization, marking the transition from legacy systems to a new, integrated SAP environment. The cut-over phase is the final step before the system is fully operational, and the process requires meticulous planning, preparation, and execution. One of the most critical components in achieving a smooth transition is having a well-organized and comprehensive Cut-Over Plan.

Image 1: Cut-Over Overview alignment with SAP Activate Phases

Cut-over activities take place during the realization, deployment, and run phases of an implementation. The cut-over execution should occur in the days leading up to the Go-Live date, but only after completing the End-to-End integration tests and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).

A cut-over plan is a detailed, step-by-step strategy designed to ensure a smooth transition from a legacy system to a new system. It outlines all the activities and tasks that need to be completed during the cut-over phase—the period right before and after the system goes live. This plan is essential to minimize disruptions, ensure data accuracy, and enable users to adopt the new system effectively.

Key Components of a Cut-Over Plan:

  1. Pre-Cut-Over Activities:
    • Data migration and validation to ensure data is accurately transferred to the new system.
    • Final system checks and testing to verify that the system is ready for Go Live.
    • Configuration verification and adjustment based on any final requirements or changes.
  2. Cut-Over Execution:
    • The specific actions to take place during the transition, such as shutting down legacy systems, migrating data, and activating the new system.
    • Timing of each activity to minimize downtime and business disruption.
  3. Post-Cut-Over Activities:
    • Monitoring system performance and ensuring stability post-Go Live.
    • Addressing any immediate issues, such as bug fixes or system optimizations.
    • Providing training and support for end-users to ensure they are comfortable with the new system.
  4. Roles and Responsibilities:
    • Clearly defines who is responsible for each task or activity during the cut-over process to ensure accountability and smooth execution.
  5. Contingency Planning:
    • Developing backup plans to handle unexpected issues or failures during the cut-over process, ensuring that the business can recover quickly.

Importance of a Cut-Over Plan:

  • Minimizes Downtime: A well-planned cut-over ensures that there is minimal disruption to business operations.
  • Ensures Data Integrity: By thoroughly checking data migration and system configurations, the plan ensures that business-critical data remains accurate.
  • Facilitates Smooth System Transition: Clear instructions on roles, tasks, and timing ensure all team members are aligned and that the Go Live happens as planned.
  • Enhances User Adoption: Post-cut-over training and support help users get up to speed quickly with the new system.

 

Image 2: Ingredients for a Successful Cut-Over

 

You want to check the available Cut-Over Template under SAP Activate Roadmap.

In short, a cut-over plan is a critical tool for managing the final stages of an implementation project, ensuring a smooth transition and successful adoption of the new system.

Tip: Don't feel overwhelmed, preparation is key, and you'll have an implementation team to guide you in planning and defining the cut-over strategy. By understanding what to expect in advance, you'll find it easier to follow the discussions and collaborate effectively with the team throughout the process.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira  or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: How to Install SSL Certificate for SAP B1 Web Browser Access

I want to install an SSL certificate for SAP Business One 10 web browser access, but I have a Public IP address. How can I do this?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: GoBD / IDW PS880 Certification for SAP ERP

Hello Danilo,

unfortunately I am still looking for it. 

And everytime I want to open your link, I got an error with the message:

entry.title: Re: SAP CTS/TMS: disable step "Report and screen generati...
entry.content:
thanks a lot. That is at least a way to try
It seems to be a way to minimize the import duration in phase "move nametab" as no one else could trigger a JIT compile blocking the import "move nametab".

There is a predecessor note 2868400 - ABAP program load generation locks during transport  
We will try and see if import times will decrease then I'll report the findings

entry.title: Re: T4/T4A, and reconciliation reports for canada payroll
entry.content:
In your DEV environment, use t-code SPRO to access the SAP Reference IMG, and use the following path:

Payroll > Payroll Canada > Reporting and Statistics > Year End Reporting > Configure wage types for Year End Reporting               You will need to configure for T424 and TA24, so look if any configuration already exists for the previous years (T4xx and TAxx)

Also look at every node under Year End Reporting (and the associated documentation).

There used to be a course given by SAP on Year End configuration, and there also are many online documents relating to "Canada Year End Configuration", or under help.sap.com (such as https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/eb65ca26f8f146c58f1fbdb013a64690/5227e153038a424de10000000a174cb4.html)

entry.title: Is it possible to reset Cumulative Received Quantities in Schedule Agreement Items every X time?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am currently analyzing a requirement with my client in order to reset Cumulative Quantities received for a specific item in a Schedule Agreement from Vendors. 

So far, I am not able to find any "**bleep**. GR Qty" anywhere in the Schedule Lines but only the "**bleep**. Sch. Qty" and the "GR Qty" specific for one delivery schedule. 

1. Is there any field available to identify the cumulative received quantity? If not, I understand we would need to calculate this based on the GR Qtities for an item, right?

2. If a valid field can be used for this, is it possible to reset it every period of time as we would do for the Agreed Cumulative Qty or is that something that can only be done for Agree Qtities?

Based on your knowlegde, I might probably need to start using the field "Agreed Cumulative Quantity" so this should be defined before it is reset as of 0 for the next period of time. Since I have never worked with this, I have an additional question:

3. By using ME88, are we only resetting this field or are we resetting actually the Cumulative Qty?

 

Thanks a lot for your inputs!!!

entry.content:
Hi All,

How to Enable Bulk download statement in Compensation Inbox.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Anyone who have encountered the configuration of CRT...
entry.content:
We had created Cumulator 1 for School Year (Sept. - Aug.), but that was mainly for Payroll purposes.

When creating (yearly) the Payroll Periods and Cumulation Periods, we had to specify in what Fiscal Year each Payroll Period was associated with.

It was Finance that controlled the Financial Year (School Year).

entry.title: 3 more videos on integrating SAP Build Apps with DMS
entry.content:
I wrote a blog about integrating Document Management Service (DMS) with SAP Build Apps, which I think will be especially helpful for those of you working on apps that involve document management.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/connect-sap-build-apps-with-dms-free-projects-videos/ba-p/13986096

The blog includes:

  • Free project
  • Links to 3 new videos showing how to connect with DMS using the new HTTP Destination Request flow function, including with the help of the free project
  • Additional tips on DMS and on using the new flow function

I’d love to hear how you’re using DMS in your own SAP Build Apps projects or any feedback you have. Feel free to join the conversation here or on the blog!

entry.title: I_Product not defind error in eclipse when creating new data definition
entry.content:
Hi ,

in second tutorial of mission - Create Value Help, Enhance the Behavior Definition and Behavior Implementation of the Shopping Cart Business Object | SAP Tutorials in steps 2 define view entity ZI_PRODUCTS_### as select from I_Product not define in sys....error is coming...

can anyone help here.....

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors and Contingent Workers
entry.content:
Hi @mnolte00 

Can you please refer the link here for employees update in LMS.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL/7b2dec4ed4d9459f893cce6f5920528d/e108986054a44fd0ab82f65b80a8774f.html?q=lms

Also please cross check the dates in Job Information. Thanks!

yes I was able to use the files when the site was set to English.

Best regards,
Michael

entry.content:
Hi @Aneta_Krysiak :

Yes, you can disable it.

Also please refer this link - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL/7b2dec4ed4d9459f893cce6f5920528d/20a5fffe87244be587b58f6ab36f1526.html?q=lms

Also you can use role based permissions to hide the same.

Please let me know if that help 🙂

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors and Contingent Workers
entry.content:
Hi @pboulicaut 
The work order ID is an external code which is auto generated by system.
You can keep it visible or hide it depending upon the business need.
 
This ID is unique ID so you cannot default this.
Thanks!

entry.content: This is also our concern for Payroll. There are some earnings in which the cumulation is from March-April and the rest are from July-June. The cumulation for July-June is working but not for March-April. This is the concern that looking which might have been solved before.

entry.title: API's for workforce management in S4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Team,


We are doing an automated sync from SuccessFactors to S4HANA for the workforce creation. Just wanted to check if we have APIs available from S4HANA workforce management as we need to implement some use cases for the IAM integration part?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: API's for workforce management in S4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Team,

You can find all the released API's in our api.sap.com repository. You could search as per the requirement,example is shown below.

https://api.sap.com/api/WORKFORCEPERSONMASTERDATAREPLI/resource

Additionally refer the below KBA which has this information.
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002905918

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/2e37a99f549a4e8880230fccbc4a6840.html

entry.title: Re: Issue with Day-Wise Depreciation Key in SAP Public Cloud

Hi Ahsan,

You need submit an incident for the development system in SAP for ME, select component XX-S4C -OPR - SRV with the subject: Create New Depreciation key if the current dep. keys do not meet your requirement.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

  • About
  • How to prepare
  • Sample questions

Please follow this step-by-step guide  to find out how to get certified.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Chart should always highlight the newest date in date range with a specific color
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Although i have already found a solution to my problem i wanted to ask the experts if there is a easier way to achieve my goal. My case i fairly simple. I have created a story filter for my date dimension. It is a filter by range with a custom filter input and the filter looks back three periods (three years in this instance). This way the user can select the year he wants and the selected year + the last three years are shown in the widget.

Now I have multiple column charts that show the amount of measure in the different year. Because of the filter the selected year + the last three year is shown.

The problem is I want a easy way to distinguish between the selected year and the last three years.

A way to do so would be to color the current year in a different way. However when the select year is changed the applied color is still applied to the same year. Therefore this method does not work.

The same problem arises if you create a color palette with three times the sames color and one time a different color. The color sequence just gets repeated. So if the data starts in 2014 the first three years are grey (2014, 2015, 2016) and the fourth is green (2017). However the next three years (2018, 2019, 2020) are grey again and so on.

As I already said I actually found already a way to achieve what i want. You would have to create two restricted measures based on the measure which should be shown in the chart. Both restricted measures are restricted by the date dimension. If you select for the values "select by range" you can select the custom input filter and therefore the selected year. For the first measure you just want the selected year. For the second measure you only want the last three years. Therefore you still select the custom input filter however you offset it by one and look back two years. This way you have a measure for the selected year and for the last three years and you can assign a color accordingly.

Although the solution explained above works. I would have to create two more restricted measures for each chart i have. Therefore I wanted to ask if someone else knows another way.

Thanks in advance for your replies

entry.title: Re: Success Factors Learning Management
entry.content: "We already know that the high number of videos in the same training item it is impacting LMS performance" - how do you mean? Videos in a single content object or multiple content objects? We do not have issues generally when there are multiple content objects. Obviously, there are some factors that need to be taken into consideration - the size and length of time to complete the content object (timeout can occur, especially when opening in a new window). We have also had to instill behavior changes such as no undocking laptops during training, network connection issues (not completely behavioral) and just leaving videos sitting to run and thus not closing the object immediately upon completion (timeout again).

entry.title: Re: Transport management
entry.content:
Hi @Chris68 
Welcome on board!
The SAP BTP Guidance Framework  provides a central access point to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP
With Learning Journeys you can build the skills you need at your own pace and embark on self-paced learning paths designed to upskill in SAP BTP.
The following might be helpful for you 

  • Learning the Basics of ABAP Programming on SAP BTP
    Have you checked our Learning Journeys? This learning journey will provide beginners with the basic ABAP programming knowledge to develop in on-premise and cloud system environments.
  • Setting up an ABAP Environment on SAP BTP
    This learning provides a step-by-step guidance for administrators and developers to set up an ABAP Environment in a productive SAP BTP Account to build cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions.

Please also check the 

  • SAP Help Portal document 
    • SAP BTP, Neo Environment 
    • Getting Started, Neo Environment
      Get onboarded in the Neo environment of SAP BTP. Follow the workflows for customer accounts or subscribe to business applications.
  • Set Up an SAP BTP Account for Tutorials 
    Get an account on SAP BTP to try out free tier service plans.  

Please let me know if this meets your inquiry.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Test Suite integration in Enhance Pace
entry.content:
With Focused Build you can use test steps during implementation. For maintenance, Enhance Pace will also be used for changes. Is it possible to continue using the existing test steps in change requests and change documents? Is there an integration of defects/defect corrections when errors occur during the tests (normal and urgent change)?

The process in Enhance Pace for defect management is not clear, so how does the Enhance Pace process deal with errors during testing?

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Price Category information
entry.content:
Hi @Tayane,

Thank you for this blog. Not sure to 100% understand what you mean about the pricing category "Individual Pricing (Add on)".

In some of the above answers, you are saying this category is "not relevant for pricing".

Wich means, if they appear in the Business Catalog list, this means they either are:

  • Activated through standard Scope Items, thus incur no extra cost
  • Activated through additional Solution Addons (such as Document and Reporting Compliance for instance), thus incur no extra cost - based on assigned Business Catalog items - either (because it has been previously added to the SAP Sales BOM for contracting, along with S/4 standard system items)

Is my understanding correct?

When I try to deploy it I am getting the error below.

Best Regards.

The details needed in the report are: 

Workflow name, Start Conditions, Workflow Steps, Recipient, etc.

Please find the screenshots below what I need in a single Report. The reason for this report is to have a overview to the business of the current setup in the system and its continued moderation.

Regards

Rahul

entry.title: Operator wants to see SFC data after completion
entry.content:
During the production process multiple data collections are done. After all production steps are done an operator is responsible to check if the data is complete. The SFC report is perfect for this but... the roles for the SFC report are to high level!!!

  • Production_Supervisor
  • Production_Engineer
  • Manufacturing_Admin
  • Global_Regional_Manager
  • Plant_Manager

We hoped we could configure the SFC report to be available for an Production_Operator or some role like this.

Any Idea how we can report all data collections for a specific SFC after completion for a production operator?

entry.title: sapgui xml file question
entry.content:
I would like to understand which file I should use in the central storage, SAPUILandscapeCore.xml or SAPUILandscape.xml or SAPUILandscapeGlobal.xml.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: sapgui xml file question
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out to SAP community. 

 

In the context of SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products) software, particularly related to the SAP GUI (Graphical User Interface), there are different configuration files that manage system connection information and landscape definitions:

  1. SAPUILandscape.xml:

    • This file typically contains the user's personalized connection settings and landscape information for logging into SAP systems. It is often utilized in scenarios where individual user preferences and custom connections need to be stored.
    • These settings are specific to the particular user and can include customized entries such as aliases and descriptions for SAP systems that the user frequently accesses.
    • The file is generally found in the user's profile directory (C:\Users\%USERPROFILE%\AppData\Roaming\SAP\Common), making it easier for each user to have their own tailored connection configurations without affecting others.

  2. SAPUILandscapeGlobal.xml:

    •  SAPUILandscapeGlobal.xml is by default in the same directory of SAPUILandscape.xml file and contains other necessary data, typically information for <Messageservers> and <Routers>, as local configuration files. Check SAP Note 2075150 - "SAP Logon (Pad) 740: New format of configuration files as of SAP GUI for Windows 7.40" for further information. 

  3. SAPUILandscapeCore.xml:

    •  This file is used when the admin want to provide an administrative core configuration file that can be supplied and set only by an administrator instead of your users. Check SAP Note 2175351 - SAP Logon (Pad): Create/distribute the administrative core configuration file in the SAP UI landscape format.

In summary, the SAPUILandscape.xml and SAPUILandscapeGlobal.xml automatically created under the user profile. In case it is desired to distribute it to multiple users, SAPUILandscape.xml is generally the one to be shared across the organization. The SAP Note 2075073 described all the process to distribute the file on server. Last but not least, the SAPUILandscapeCore.xml is also used as a server configuration file, however it can be set  only by administrator.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: how to display a pdf document within a container?
entry.content:
Hi Builders !

I am testing the functionalities of SAP Build Apps and I could not find a way to display a pdf document in a container (embedded). I succeeded to open the pdf with a modal window but I was not able to display it directly within the app.

Example: on the left, I have a pick up file process that allow me to select the pdf from my local repository and I would like to display the selected pdf in the container on the right.

I try with base64 conversion but no success... Any tips? 


Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025

entry.title: Re: Modeling a basic Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @jannis that's hard to say without seeing the actual models you are using. Did you try creating a new Analytic Model?

entry.title: BYD2411 Project Invoice Request
entry.content:
We cannot create a new Project Invoice Request


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2411 Project Invoice Request
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

If you go to Customer Invoicing work center - Project Invoicing- Project Invoice Requests view - No records available.

Basically project invoicing is a process that allows you to invoice customers for project-based time and expenses or fixed-price services. You can only perform end-to-end project invoicing for customer projects since they are the only project types that are integrated with sales orders. You can check the activity type - 'Project Types' in Fine Tune activity to check which of your project types represent customer projects. 

--Cause--

Prerequisites
- A customer project has been created and released.
- Project type must be of type - Customer project with sales integration
- One or more sales orders for the same customer and within the same company have been created and released. All sales order items with project-based item types are assigned to project tasks of the customer project.

You can use Customer Project variant type for projects which you manage and execute for your customers. These projects must always be linked to or created from a corresponding sales order. The system handles project costs and revenues as Cost of Goods Sold and Sales Revenues. This integrates these projects into the standard sales scenario, which offers functions such as reporting by function of expense, profit analysis, and automatic revenue recognition.

When I checked in Project Management work center - Projects view - Customer project with sales integration - I do not see any projects integrated with sales order. 

--Solution--

In Business configuration work center - Implementation Projects - Edit Project Scope - Scoping - Select Project Management - Project Planning and Execution - I see Expense Recording is not selected. The Expense Recording business topic allows project team members to record miscellaneous and travel expenses. The project team member can then assign the expenses to either projects or cost centers. 

Then you need to create Project Type - Customer project with sales integration and make sure that, sales order is created with project assignment. 

Once these data is available, you can create project invoice requests for customer projects in which you invoice the customer for time and expenses spent on the project or for fixed price services. 

Best Regards,
Arun

entry.title: Re: Missing Execution ID in SAP AI Launchpad After Connec...
entry.content: Thank you so much!

entry.title: Entities Created in SAP CAP UI but Fields Have Null Values and Data Not Inserted in the DB
entry.content:
You create an entity in the UI, fill in the required fields, and submit the form. The entity object is successfully created, but when checking the result, you see the entity with all fields set to null. Moreover, when checking the database, you discover that the data hasn't actually been inserted.

 

this is my entity in schema.cds : 

entity SegmentList : managed
{
    key ID : UUID;
    description : String(100) ;
    segment : String(100);
    qualifier : String(100);
    qualifier_value : String(100);
    field : String(100);
    rule_for_country : String(100);
    scope : String(100);
    notification_type : String(100);
    idocTypeConfiguration : Association to one IdocTypeConfiguration;
   
}   
 
 
this is my entity in annotations.cds :
annotate service.SegmentList with @(
    UI.LineItem #Segment : [


        {  
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.description,
            Label : 'description',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.segment,
            Label : 'segment',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.qualifier,
            Label : 'qualifier',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.qualifier_value,
            Label : 'qualifier_value',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.field,
            Label : 'field',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.rule_for_country,
            Label : 'rule_for_country',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.scope,
            Label : 'scope',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : idocTypeConfiguration.segList.notification_type,
            Label : 'notification_type',
        },
    ]
);

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Check not working after SP31 patch
entry.content:
SAP Note 3292392 (SFTPDuplicate Check default changes) mentions that the duplicate check is not valid in “test mode” and defines test mode as being when “delete file” and “archive file” are both unchecked.

We recently had the same issue as you and our solution was to enable "Archiving on File System" in the advanced tab and archive the files to any location.

It isn't obvious in the note that this is new functionality and it took us a while to determine this was our issue.

entry.title: Hybrid landscape with RISE
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a customer with many ERP systems across the globe. He has currently more than 5 ECC systems that are  technically disparate and in different countries . He would like to go to S/4HANA with a greenfield approach and go to RISE with SAP . But for security reasons he can not put all his systems in the cloud and one system should remain on premises.

  He would like to keep the development and quality environment in RISE and most of pre-production and production in RISE, but one pre-production and one production would go on-premises (as described in the image). Do you know if it's possible to have an hybrid landscape, with most of the systems in RISE in different countries and some systems on-premises ? How the basis part (TOs, refreshes, upgrades...) is managed by SAP in such cases ?

entry.title: PEO - Can't complete Buyoff
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I am currently trying to configure and run PEO on our test system. For configuration I followed this guide here:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/s-4hana-extended-production-engineering-and-operations-peo-part-1/ba-p/13454624

I created a shop floor routing that has a buyoff, but when trying to claim the buyoff in the respective app, I get an error message saying that i dont have the necessary qualification to do so, even though it is assigned to me in HR. Anybody faced the same issue or got an idea what is missing?

entry.title: Azure, IAS, and Cloud Apps Authentication
entry.content:
Hi All, Hi @Matthew_Shaw  

@Matthew_Shaw thank you so much for your help!

Now a clarification question: We have Azure as IdP, IAS as a proxy, and cloud apps like SAC configured. We provision users from Azure to IAS and then to SAC.

Ideally, we would like to log in to the apps  (like SAC) using an employee number (e.g., 283268).

I can of course provision the employee number (283268) from Azure to IAS in the "Login Name" field and then to SAP SAC in the "USER ID". However, when a user tries to log in to SAC, they cannot log in with the employee number (283268). This user is not found in Azure. If the user logs in with the email address, the login works. What am I doing wrong here?

Question 1: Is it possible to authenticate in cloud apps (such as SAC) using an employee number from Azure (since users are redirected to Azure for authentication)?

Question 2: In Azure, the UPN is set as the email address, e.g., firstname.lastname@mail.com. If I form the username "f.lastname" from firstname.lastname@mail.com and transfer it from Azure -> IAS -> SAC, can the user log in with "f.lastname"? Will this user be found in Azure with "f.lastname"? What is the best attribute from Azure to provision to the USER ID for authentication?

Thank you again. Unfortunately, I am stuck here and need help.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Trial Integration Suite in Singapore has deployment i...
entry.content: Yes, from my side, as well. Some services in the tenant work node were not getting activated, apparently

entry.title: Cloud identity service user created failed login to an application
entry.content:
After following the guide (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/create-openid-connect-application) and having established a trust connection to one of my applications in cloud identity service  , creating a new user and assigning it to a group of type (usergroup and creating the respective rule in Conditional Authentication) for my application, when I try to login via default identity provider I get "
Sorry, we could not authenticate you. Try again."

Please note that with an admin account I can easily log in.

I also tried without creating the group but nothing changes.

How can I resolve this issue?

thanks,

Davide

entry.title: Standard and Actual Costing for Multiple Valuation type with Split Valuated Material
entry.content:
Hello Gurus, 

We have a requirement to do implement a process of standard costing and actual costing in a scenario where the stock is manufactured in a plant say P001 and every process order is bit unique to other depending on the raw material available at the time of manufacturing hence when the goods receipt is done every process order is assigned with a different batch number. In this plant P001 we can do the standard costing based on the standard Master recipe - there would be a variance in the order depending on which alternative BOM is used which is ok but now these different batches are transferred to distribution plant D001 from where it gets sold to customer - when these batches get transferred to D001 in current ECC system client creates a different valuation type for every batch dynamically during every transfer (STO) - now the issue - is there any functionality in the standard costing that these batches get standard cost in plant D001 for every transfer or we have to keep the cost at moving average price for these materials in plant D001. 

Next question is how the actual costing CKMLCP will behave for the variance between P001 and D001 if the price at D001 is at moving average price. Will we be able to get different prices for every batch at plant D001 depending on the different batches good receipted in plant P001.

I understand there is a transaction OMWC (refer note 1664797) where I can do the standard costing for split valuated material, but we can only fix it for one valuation type only but in our case, we have multiple valuation type for every batch so I am assuming it will not work for us. 

Any suggestions from finance community. 

Thanks in Advance.

Rohit
